{"key_event":"Constitutional consolidation under the new federal government","reasoning":"Civil society was already vigorous among enfranchised white men, with active newspapers, parties, churches, local assemblies, and mobilization such as opposition to taxes, but exclusion of enslaved people, women, Indigenous peoples, and many poorer citizens limits the overall score. The new federal state gained real authority under the Constitution, created executive departments, courts, revenue mechanisms, and suppressed the Whiskey Rebellion, yet its bureaucracy, fiscal base, army, and territorial reach remained modest by later standards.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":6.5,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":5.0}
